title: Short trades are treated as long trades in profitable-count and log analyses

evidence.quote: enter with buy ... exit with sell ... Profit: $%s

reasoning: The package explicitly supports short positions, and TotalProfitAnalysis computes short PnL as cost basis minus exit value. ProfitableTradesAnalysis ignores position direction, so a profitable short entry SELL 10 exit BUY 5 is not counted, while a losing short exit BUY 15 is counted. LogTradesAnalysis also hard-codes buy/sell wording and logs exit-minus-cost profit, so short trade logs report the wrong side labels and inverted profit sign.
recommendation: Add a direction-aware PnL helper or branch on trade.IsLong()/trade.IsShort() in ProfitableTradesAnalysis and LogTradesAnalysis. Use the actual EntranceOrder and ExitOrder side labels when formatting logs.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Existing LogTradesAnalysis and ProfitableTradesAnalysis tests only create BUY entries followed by SELL exits, so the short-side path is never exercised.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add short-position cases for ProfitableTradesAnalysis and LogTradesAnalysis, e.g. SELL 1 @ 10 followed by BUY 1 @ 5 should count as profitable and log positive profit with sell entry/buy exit.
minimumFixScope: Update ProfitableTradesAnalysis.Analyze and LogTradesAnalysis.Analyze in pkg/analysis/analysis.go, plus focused short-trade tests in pkg/analysis/analysis_test.go.
